purpose: Placeholder manifest for the first source-level contract-test families.

families.goal: Lock the minimum host strategy shell required by reused order, fill, exit, and safety components.
Ensure exit submissions stay centralized and no competing exit path is introduced.
Protect the single-source-of-truth execution/fill lane and parity-relevant recovery/coverage sequencing without binding to a specific transport implementation.
Keep the donor runtime import behind explicit host-shell, lifecycle, and activation TODO seams until the host wiring is complete.
Lock the host-state surface needed for later adoption of the external runtime harness.
Freeze the harness-aligned runtime snapshot/diagnostic surface and the parity-relevant gating/coverage/finalization semantics it exposes.
Lock the first external runtime-harness scenario pack and its semantic anchors before adapter work begins.
Freeze the thin local harness-adapter surface used to drive the first restart and compatible-coverage scenarios.
Freeze the flat harness-shaped snapshot projection that external runtime scenarios will eventually consume.
Catch parity-relevant durable and trade-scoped state drift whenever recovery, stop, or coverage fields are added or renamed.
Freeze the real Debug/Risk/Patterns/Trades logging sinks and the entry-observation surface that explains setup progression and block reasons.
Keep the playback strategy on a donor-style simple-trail exit lane instead of drifting back to mixed target-plus-trail management.
Freeze the stripped-down v1 entry surface so deferred HTF/RVOL/opening-bias logic does not drift back into the base strategy.
Keep checklist, runbooks, and artifact templates aligned so compile/playback/harness signoff evidence stays explicit and reviewable.
